generated_oml:
instance PhysicalSystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Active component in the Digital Twin consumes real‑time data from the physical system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RealTimeData
]

instance RealTimeData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PhysicalSystemSensor
]

instance RealTimeMeasurementData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real‑time measurements and predictive information processed by the Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnvironment, CodeGenPipeline
]

instance SimulinkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink simulation environment used to refine behavioral aspects of the model"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance CodeGenPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Code generation pipeline mapping design artifacts to C++ or Java code"
    DTDFVocab:enables CodeGenerationService
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that runs simulations in Simulink to validate the model"
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelValidationInsight
]

instance CodeGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that generates executable code from SysML/ADL artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:provides CodeGenerationAction
]

instance ModelValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight that the model has been validated against simulation results"
]

instance CodeGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action that updates the physical system with freshly generated code"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "Lifecycle stages for a Digital Twin are understood through MBSE as a sequence of abstraction layers: requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers. Transitions between these layers are managed via SysML models (requirements, behavioral diagrams, IBDs) and corresponding ADLs (ACME, AADL, MontiArc) that capture component‑connector relationships and state machine behavior. Governance of the lifecycle is implicit in the MBSE process, with architecture description languages ensuring traceability from high‑level functional requirements down to executable code representations."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Adherence to the SysML modeling language (ISO/IEC 59556) is implicit, as SysML is used for requirements and architecture modeling. Component/connector descriptions may use the ACME or AADL standards, and state machine behavior can be modeled with MontiArc. The document does not specify additional certification or compliance frameworks beyond these modeling standards. Interoperability is supported through the use of open architecture description languages (e.g., ACME, AADL) that facilitate mapping between different system views."
]
